slides.title: Introduction to Linear Regression
slides.content: Linear regression is a fundamental algorithm in machine learning and statistics. It predicts a continuous value as an output based on a given input.

slides.title: Simple Linear Regression
slides.content:
Equation: y = mx + c
- m: Slope of the line
- c: y-intercept

visual_assets.description: A graph showing a straight line fit on data points, illustrating simple linear regression

slides.title: Understanding R-squared
slides.content: R-squared measures the proportion of the variance for a dependent variable that's explained by an independent variable.

visual_assets.description: A chart comparing R-squared scores for different models, highlighting the goodness of fit

slides.title: Multiple Linear Regression
slides.content:
Expands on simple linear regression by predicting the output using multiple features.
Equation: y = b0 + b1x1 + b2x2 + ... + bnxn

visual_assets.description: A plane fitting through points in a 3D space representing multiple linear regression

slides.title: Polynomial Regression
slides.content:
An extension of linear regression for capturing the non-linear relationship by adding polynomial terms.
Equation: y = b0 + b1x + b2x^2 + ... + bnx^n

visual_assets.description: Curved line fitting data points better than a straight line, illustrating polynomial regression

slides.title: Python Code Example
slides.content: Using Scikit-Learn to fit a linear regression model

visual_assets.code:
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
model = LinearRegression()
model.fit(X_train, y_train)
visual_assets.caption: Fitting a simple linear regression model

slides.title: Conclusion
slides.content: Linear regression serves as a baseline model in machine learning. It provides insights into the relationships between variables and can be scaled to complex models.
